Items.Title: 2023 年排名前 5 的 React 管理仪表板库 | Top 5 React Admin Dashboard Libraries in 2023
Items.Description:
添加一名作者

介绍

管理仪表板在 B2B 业务的成功中起着至关重要的作用。 这些仪表板旨在提供关键绩效指标、数据准确性和管理操作的个性化概览，所有这些都会对企业管理的决策过程产生重大影响，无论是积极的还是消极的。

由于管理仪表板在任何严肃业务的成功中发挥着至关重要的作用，因此开发人员经常承担将这些工具集成到网站中的任务。 然而，创建有效的 React 管理仪表板并非易事，因为它需要广泛使用高级数据管理、数据可视化和路由系统以确保功能正常。 对于那些时间有限的人来说，使用库快速引导仪表板应用程序可能是首选。

这将我们引向下一个挑战：为您的应用程序选择合适的 React 管理仪表板库。 选择 React 管理仪表板库涉及许多考虑因素，虽然可视化很重要，但它并不是唯一的关键因素。 一个优质的仪表板库不仅应该具有视觉吸引力，还应该与各种后端技术无缝集成、响应迅速并具备其他基本功能。

在本文中，我们精选了 5 个最好的 React 管理仪表板库，其中包含以最少的努力创建现代且功能丰富的仪表板所需的所有基本功能。

提炼

位于列表顶部的是refine ，这是一个全面的开源库，用于开发数据驱动的 React 管理仪表板，具有您通常在其他仪表板库中找不到的功能。

类似于几个基于 React 的管理仪表板库， refine是建立在 React 框架上的。 然而，refine 不是一个独立的元框架，允许它在任何能够运行 React 的环境中运行，包括 CRA、Next.js、Remix、Vite 等。

这让 refine 可以利用这些框架必须提供的广泛功能，包括对 SSR 的支持以及 React 生态系统中的各种高级状态管理、数据管理和路由技术——例如 React Query、React Router 或 Next.js路由系统和 React Hook Form。 这使得 refine 能够构建具有直观和视觉吸引力的界面的感知分析反应管理面板和仪表板应用程序，配备了执行 CRUD 操作以及许多其他功能的能力。

将 refine 与其他管理库区分开来的一个独特功能是其无头设计。 该属性允许它与自定义设计或用户界面 (UI) 库无缝集成，例如Material UI 、 Mantine 、 Ant Design和Chakra 。 因此，您可以根据自己的喜好和业务模型完全个性化应用程序的外观。

此外，refine 提供了一系列 helper hooks、组件和数据提供程序，它们提供对应用程序用户界面的完全控制，并且可以轻松适应不同的后端架构，如 GraphQL、REST 和 SOAP，并实现无缝身份验证和访问控制流。

特征

  • 开源
  • 与 UI 无关
  • 一流的 Typescript 支持
  • 后端不可知
  • 验证
  • 简单的学习曲线
  • 国际化 (I18n)
  • 辅助功能
  • 增删改查操作

快速开始

npm create refine-app#latest my-project

统计数据

  • 许可证：麻省理工学院
  • 链接：演示| 文档| 该库基于 Ant Design 原则，包括更高级别的各种预构建组件、布局和设计套件。 这使得它具有极强的适应性、广泛的文档记录并且易于学习。

    由于 Ant Design 由阿里巴巴集团拥有和管理，因此该库会不断更新和维护以确保它是最新的。 这意味着您可以对库的安全性充满信心，并将精力集中在构建一流的应用程序上。

    特征

    • 国际化 (I18n)
    • 主题
    • 预设风格
    • 响应能力
    • 打字稿支持
    • 开源
    • 简单的学习曲线

    快速开始

    npm i @ant-design/pro-cli -g pro create my-app

    统计数据

    • 许可证：麻省理工学院
    • 链接：演示| 文档| 震颤

      它提供模块化组件，可以组合这些组件以构建高度定制的仪表板或分析界面。

      这些组件可以通过原型设计和按适当的顺序组织它们来快速组合和排列，对精确校准的要求最低。

      作为首批采用 Tailwind 作为其主要主题系统的仪表板库之一，Tremor 的组件具有令人惊叹的内置样式。 然而，该库还允许使用原生 CSS 作为辅助插件来管理次要的布局方面。

      特征

      • 快速的工作流程
      • 响应能力
      • 简单的学习曲线
      • 开源

      快速开始

      npm install @tremor/react //install tailwind and it's peer dependencies npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer npx tailwindcss init -p

      统计数据

      链接：演示| 文档| 材料仪表板 React

      该库提供一组 UI 组件和预构建页面，以帮助开发人员快速创建具有专业外观的管理面板和仪表板。

      该模板包括各种UI组件，例如表格、图表、表单和卡片，可以轻松用于构建复杂的界面。 它还提供多种配色方案和自定义选项，允许开发人员根据项目需要定制仪表板。

      特征

      • 响应能力
      • 验证
      • 主题
      • 简单的学习曲线

      快速开始

      npm i material-dashboard-react

      统计数据

      链接： 演示| 文档| 电压反应

      它提供了一个包含 100 多个元素的简洁现代 UI，其中包括小部件、图表和数据可视化工具，可帮助开发人员构建功能强大且响应迅速的管理面板。
      通过一组对象，该库可以在预构建组件（包括表单元素和其他 UI 元素）之间高效地传输数据。 这使得在 React 管理仪表板中轻松集成和自定义数据显示成为可能。

      特征

      • 响应能力
      • 创意资产权利
      • 快速开始
        //clone the repo git clone https://github.com/themesberg/volt-react-dashboard.git //then Yarn start Or npm run start

        统计数据

        • 许可证：麻省理工学院
        • 链接：演示| 文档| 结论

          您可能已经注意到，本文中提到的一些库是付费模板的免费版本。 虽然可以通过添加所需功能来增强这些开源库，但它们可能无法满足您的需求。 因此，如果您正在寻找一个可以免费帮助您创建具有所有必要功能的高级 React 管理仪表板的库，我建议您考虑 refine 或 Tremor。
          但是，如果您希望快速创建最小的东西，那么提到的其余库就足够了。 最终，这取决于您的特定项目要求和复杂性。

          一旦暂停，refine 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

          解除暂停后，refine 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

          一旦取消发布，refine 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

          如果 refine 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

          笔记：

          感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记精炼的方法：

          通过优化使所有帖子不那么明显

          报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的精炼将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Author: David Omotayo

Introduction

Admin dashboards play a crucial role in the success of B2B businesses. These dashboards are designed to provide a personalized overview of key performance indicators, data accuracy, and administrative actions, all of which have a significant impact on the decision-making process of a business administration, either positively or negatively.

Due to the crucial role played by admin dashboards in the success of any serious business, developers are often tasked with integrating these tools into websites. However, creating effective React admin dashboards is no small feat, as it requires extensive use of advanced data management, data visualization, and routing systems to ensure proper functionality. For those with limited time on their hands, using a library to quickly bootstrap a dashboard application may be the preferred option.

This leads us to the next challenge: selecting the appropriate React admin dashboard library for your application. Choosing a React admin dashboard library involves many considerations, and while visualization is important, it is not the only critical factor. A quality dashboard library should not only be visually attractive, but also integrate seamlessly with various backend technologies, be responsive, and possess other essential features.

In this article, we've curated a compilation of the 5 best React admin dashboard libraries that encompass all the essential features needed to create modern and feature-rich dashboards with minimal effort.

refine

At the top of the list is refine, a comprehensive, open-source library for developing data-driven React admin dashboards with features you wouldn’t typically find in other dashboard libraries.

Similar to several React-based admin dashboard libraries, refine is built on the React framework. However, refine is not a self-contained meta-framework, allowing it to function in any environment capable of running React, including CRA, Next.js, Remix, Vite, and others.

This lets refine leverage the wide range of features these frameworks has to offer, including support for SSR as well as various advanced state management, data management, and routing technologies within the React ecosystem - such as React Query, React Router or the Next.js routing system, and React Hook Form. This enables refine to construct perceptive analytic react admin panels and dashboard applications with intuitive and visually appealing interfaces, equipped with the ability to perform CRUD operations amongst many other functionalities.

One unique feature distinguishing refine from other admin libraries is its headless design. This attribute allows it to seamlessly integrate with custom design or user interface (UI) libraries, such as Material UI, Mantine, Ant Design, and Chakra. Consequently, you can fully personalize the appearance of your application according to your preferences and business model.

Additionally, refine provides an array of helper hooks, components, and data providers that provide complete control over your application's user interface and can easily adapt to different backend architectures like GraphQL, REST, and SOAP, and for seamless authentication and access control flow.

Features

  • Open-source
  • UI-agnostic
  • SSR support
  • First-class Typescript support
  • Backend-agnostic
  • Authentication
  • Easy learning curve
  • Internationalization ( I18n )
  • Accessibility
  • CRUD operations

Quick start 

npm create refine-app#latest my-project

Stats

  • GitHub stars: 8.5k
  • License: MIT
  • Links: Demo | Documentation | GitHub

Ant Design pro

Ant design pro is an open-source UI library for building production-ready enterprise-level web applications and React admin dashboards.

The library is based on the Ant Design principles and includes a higher level variety of pre-built components, layouts, and design kits. This makes it extremely adaptable, extensively documented, and easy to learn.

Since Ant Design is owned and managed by the Alibaba Group, the library undergoes constant updates and maintenance to ensure it is up-to-date. This means that you can be confident in the library's security and focus your efforts on building top-notch applications.

Features

  • Internationalization ( I18n )
  • Theming
  • Preset style
  • Responsiveness
  • Typescript support
  • Open-source
  • Easy learning curve

Quick start 

npm i @ant-design/pro-cli -g
pro create my-app

Stats

  • Github stars: 33.8k
  • License: MIT
  • Links: Demo | Documentation | Github

Tremor

Tremor is an open-source library for quickly building insightful React admin dashboards. It provides modular components that can be combined to build highly customized dashboards or analytic interfaces.

Tremor is an opinionated and low-level UI library that provides a variety of components, including layouts, charts, texts, and cards. These components can be quickly combined and arranged by prototyping and organizing them in the appropriate sequence, with minimal requirement for exact calibration.

As one of the first dashboard libraries to adopt Tailwind as its primary theming system, Tremor's components come with stunning built-in styles. Yet, the library also permits the utilization of native CSS as supplementary add-ons for managing minor layout aspects.

Features

  • Fast workflow
  • Responsiveness
  • Easy learning curve
  • Open source

Quick start 

npm install @tremor/react
//install tailwind and it’s peer dependencies
npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer 
npx tailwindcss init -p

Stats

Github stars: 6.6k License: MIT

Links: Demo | Documentation | GitHub

Material Dashboard React

Material Dashboard React is an open-source admin dashboard template built using React and the material design framework.

The library provides a set of UI components and pre-built pages to help developers create professional-looking admin panels and dashboards quickly.

The template includes various UI components, such as tables, charts, forms, and cards, which can easily be used to build complex interfaces. It also offers multiple color schemes and customization options, allowing developers to tailor the dashboard to their project's needs.

Features

  • Responsiveness
  • Authentication
  • Theming
  • Easy learning curve

Quick start 

npm i material-dashboard-react

Stats

Github stars: 2.4k License: MIT

Links: Demo | Documentation | GitHub

Volt React

Volt is an open-source admin dashboard template built with Bootstrap 5 and designed with simplicity and ease-of-use in mind.

It provides a clean and modern UI with over 100 elements, which includes widgets, charts, and data visualization tools to help developers build powerful and responsive admin panels.
Volt’s structural design simplifies the visualization of data acquired from backend technologies. Through an array of objects, the library efficiently transfers data between pre-built components, including form elements and other UI elements. This enables easy integration and customization of data display within the React admin dashboard.

Features

  • WCAG Accessibility
  • Responsiveness
  • Creative asset rights
  • Mapbox integration
  • W3C validated pages

Quick start 

//clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/themesberg/volt-react-dashboard.git 
//then 
Yarn start
Or
npm run start

 Stats

  • Github stars: 728
  • License: MIT
  • Links: Demo | Documentation | GitHub



Conclusion

You may have noticed that some of the libraries mentioned in this article are free versions of paid templates. While these open-source libraries can be enhanced by adding required features, they may not meet your needs. Therefore, if you are searching for a library that can assist you in creating advanced React admin dashboards with all necessary features for free, I would recommend considering refine or Tremor.
However, If you are looking to create something minimal quickly, the rest of the libraries mentioned would suffice. Ultimately, it depends on your specific project requirements and complexity.

Once suspended, refine will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, refine will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by refine will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If refine is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag refine:

Make all posts by refine less visible

refine consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging refine will restore default visibility to their posts.

Items.Title: 使用泛型进行分页的可重用组件存储 | Reusable component store for pagination using generics
Items.Description:
在上一篇文章中，我们使用 NgRx 创建了一个组件存储来处理待办事项的分页

不幸的是，这种方法非常适合我们的领域，现在是我们寻找可重用解决方案的时候了，我们可以将其应用到我们希望以相同方式分页的另一个实体上

表中的内容

创建我们的新商店

让我们开始创建一个新的paginated-items.component-store.ts文件，我们的
分页逻辑将发生。

国家

在定义我们的商店之前，我们首先必须关注它的状态

这是我们当前形式的状态：

 export interface AppState { todoItems: TodoItem[]; offset: number; pageSize: number; }

我们可以将其属性分为两大类：

  • 与分页相关的一切（ offset ， pageSize ）
  • 与项目本身相关的一切（在我们的例子中只有todoItems ）

由此，我们可以提取出一个后续接口，负责包装分页相关的参数：

 export interface PaginationDetails { offset: number; pageSize: number; }

另一个将包装页面内容：

 export interface PageContent { todoItems: TodoItem[]; }

使用这两个接口，我们现在有一个更明确的状态：

 export interface PaginatedItemsState { paginationDetails: PaginationDetails; pageContent: PageContent; }

太好了，让我们继续！

商店本身

奠定基础

这个store将被定义为一个抽象类，这样每个后续的store都可以在is中添加自己的逻辑。

使用之前定义的状态，我们可以这样写：

 @Injectable() // 👇 Beware not to forget the `abstract` here export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> { }

创建选择器

拥有状态管理很棒，能够访问其内容更好：让我们添加一些选择器。

我们可以从添加一些基础的开始，以检索每个属性：

 @Injectable() export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> { readonly selectPaginatedItemsState = this.select((state) => state); readonly selectPaginationDetails = this.select( this.selectPaginatedItemsState, ({ paginationDetails }) => paginationDetails ); readonly selectOffset = this.select( this.selectPaginationDetails, ({ offset }) => offset ); readonly selectPageSize = this.select( this.selectPaginationDetails, ({ pageSize }) => pageSize ); readonly selectPageContent = this.select( this.selectPaginatedItemsState, ({ pageContent }) => pageContent ); readonly selectTodoItems = this.select( this.selectPageContent, ({ todoItems }) => todoItems ); }

这是很多选择器，但请记住，这个商店的目的是足够灵活，以便任何继承自它的商店都可以使用这些内部结构，而不是在其他地方重写它们

处理分页

从我们的商店中，让我们添加一些更新程序来更新我们的状态：

 @Injectable() export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> { /* Selectors omitted here */ private readonly updatePagination = this.updater( (state, paginationDetails: PaginationDetails) => ({ ...state, paginationDetails }) ); private readonly updatePaginatedItems = this.updater( (state, pageContent: PageContent) => ({ ...state, pageContent }) ); }

我将使用一个用于分页，一个用于分页项目，但如果您愿意，可以或多或少地使用粒度！

最后，我们可以从AppComponentStore复制、粘贴和调整我们之前的两个loadPage和loadNextPage效果：

 @Injectable() export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> { /* Selectors omitted here */ // 👇 Don't forget to also inject our service private readonly _todoItemService = inject(TodoItemService); readonly loadPage = this.effect((trigger$: Observable<void>) => { return trigger$.pipe( // 👇 We can directly access our pagination details from our selector withLatestFrom(this.selectPaginationDetails), switchMap(([, { offset, pageSize }]) => this._todoItemService.getTodoItems(offset, pageSize).pipe( tapResponse( (todoItems: TodoItem[]) => this.updatePaginatedItems({ todoItems }), () => console.error("Something went wrong") ) ) ) ); }); readonly loadNextPage = this.effect((trigger$: Observable<void>) => { return trigger$.pipe( // 👇 Same here withLatestFrom(this.selectPaginationDetails), tap(([, { offset, pageSize }]) => this.updatePagination({ offset: offset + pageSize, pageSize })), tap(() => this.loadPage()) ); }); /* Updaters omitted here */ }

使用 NgRx 生命周期钩子

正如我们在第一个版本中所做的那样，我们还可以利用此处的OnStoreInit生命周期挂钩在启动时加载页面：

 @Injectable() export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> implements OnStoreInit { ngrxOnStoreInit() { this.loadPage(); } }

通过这样做，我们可以确保每个存储分页项目，从而继承这个项目，将在创建时加载第一页

从我们的商店继承

最后，剩下要做的就是让我们的AppComponentStore继承我们的PaginatedItemsComponentStore而不是ComponentStore<AppState>

为此，我们需要更改初始状态并摆脱我们自己的AppState以使用提供的PaginatedItemsState ：

 // app.component-store.ts const initialState: PaginatedItemsState = { paginationDetails: { offset: 0, pageSize: 10, }, pageContent: { todoItems: [], }, };

完成后，我们可以从组件存储中删除所有更新程序、效果和生命周期挂钩实现，并改用PaginatedComponentStore的逻辑：

 @Injectable() export class AppComponentStore extends PaginatedItemsComponentStore { readonly vm$ = this.select( this.selectTodoItems, (todoItems) => ({ todoItems })); constructor() { super(initialState); } }

如果您在此更改后运行您的应用程序，一切都应该像以前一样工作，耶！

引入泛型

到目前为止一切顺利，但让我们不要高兴得太快

如果一切就像将代码从我们以前的商店复制粘贴到新商店一样简单，那只是因为我们将其限制为TodoItem s

在常规应用程序中，您可能（肯定）有不止一种类型的实体要分页

幸运的是，TypeScript 可以很好地处理泛型，我们可以利用这一点来提高PaginatedItemsComponentStore的可重用性

重做我们的状态

首先要看的地方就是我们的PageContent接口

在这一个中，我们将内容定义为TodoItem的数组，但我们希望允许更广泛的类型集

我们可能想将其更改为any[]但由于我们使用的是 _Type_Script 而不是 _Any_Script，我们可能会找到更好的方法

使用泛型，我们可以向我们的接口指定我们想要一个类型为TItem的项目数组，因为我们还不知道它：

 export interface PageContent<TItem> { // 👇 Since we are manipulating items now I renamed the property items: TItem[]; }

按照惯例，我通过以T开头后跟其逻辑含义来命名任何泛型类型

更新这个接口也需要我们重写PaginatedItemsState ，因为我们需要传播泛型：

 export interface PaginatedItemsState<TItem> { paginationDetails: PaginationDetails; pageContent: PageContent<TItem>; }

更新我们的商店

随着对状态的更新，我们的商店不再有效，我们还需要在这里传播通用类型

但是，我们还不想使用具体类型，否则我们所有的修改都会白费

为了解决第一个编译错误，我们首先还需要指出我们将使用TItem商店：

 @Injectable() export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore<TItem> extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState<TItem>> implements OnStoreInit { /* ... */ }

这样做之后，我们需要解决两个小错误：

  • 在我们的selectTodoItems选择器中， todoItems不再存在，因为我们已将其重命名为items 。 我们可以通过更改属性名称来修复它：
 readonly selectItems = this.select( this.selectPageContent, ({ items }) => items );
  • 在我们更新的updatePaginatedItems中， PageContent不知道泛型类型，我们需要指定它：
 private readonly updatePaginatedItems = this.updater( (state, pageContent: PageContent<TItem>) => ({ ...state, pageContent }) );

但是，我们现在面临一个更大的问题：在loadPage效果中，我们正在调用todoItemService并且此服务非常特定于我们的TodoItem s

委托抓取逻辑

从我们的PaginatedItemsComponentStore中，我们无法提前知道在给定偏移量和页面大小的情况下如何检索特定类型的TItem

但是，将知道的类是实现类

幸运的是，我们在一个抽象类中，我们可以通过添加一个抽象方法让子类定义自己的逻辑：

 protected abstract getItems(paginationDetails: PaginationDetails): Observable<TItem[]>;

使用这个方法，我们现在可以删除我们服务的实例并用抽象方法替换它的调用：

 - private readonly _todoItemService = inject(TodoItemService); readonly loadPage = this.effect((trigger$: Observable<void>) => { return trigger$.pipe( withLatestFrom(this.selectPaginationDetails), switchMap(([, { offset, pageSize }]) => - this._todoItemService.getTodoItems(offset, pageSize).pipe( + this.getItems({ offset, pageSize }).pipe( tapResponse( - (todoItems: TodoItem[]) => this.updatePaginatedItems({ todoItems }), + (items: TItem[]) => this.updatePaginatedItems({ items }), () => console.error("Something went wrong") ) ) ) ); });

更新AppComponentStore

我们快完成了！ 现在我们的基础商店是通用的，我们需要在AppComponentStore中指定TItem对我们来说将是TodoItem

 // 👇 Notice that we are now talking about `TodoItem` const initialState: PaginatedItemsState<TodoItem> = { paginationDetails: { offset: 0, pageSize: 10, }, pageContent: { items: [], }, }; @Injectable() export class AppComponentStore // 👇 Same here extends PaginatedItemsComponentStore<TodoItem> { readonly vm$ = this.select( // 👇 Don't forget that our selector has been renamed this.selectItems, (todoItems) => ({ todoItems })); }

但是，我们现在还需要实现该getItems方法，以便我们的父组件知道如何检索那些TodoItem s

为此，我们需要重新注入一个TodoItemService实例并从那里调用它：

 private readonly _todoItemService = inject(TodoItemService); protected getItems({ offset, pageSize }: PaginationDetails): Observable<TodoItem[]> { return this._todoItemService.getTodoItems(offset, pageSize); }

再次构建我们的应用程序，一切都应该像以前一样工作，但是这一次，对新类型的实体进行分页不需要您再次重写整个组件存储！

在本文中，我们了解了如何利用泛型将常见的分页逻辑提升为一个抽象组件，我们可以在以后对其进行扩展

如果您想更进一步，您可以尝试：

  • 处理加载和错误逻辑
  • 添加额外的选择器（页面的第一项等）
  • 创建一个与TodoItemService几乎相同的新PostService ，只是它通过调用https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts来检索帖子。 然后，您可以通过定义继承自PaginatedItemsComponentStore<Post>的新组件存储，使用此服务对Post而非TodoItem进行分页

如果您想检查生成的代码，可以前往相关的GitHub 存储库

我希望您在那里学到了一些有用的东西，并且一如既往地祝您编码愉快！

照片由Roman Trifonov在Unsplash上拍摄



原文:

In the last article we created a component store using NgRx to handle the pagination of todo items

Unfortunately, this approach is very specific to our domain and it is now time for us to look for a reusable solution that we can slap on another entity that we would like to paginate the same way

Table of content

Creating our new store

Let's begin by creating a new paginated-items.component-store.ts file where our
pagination logic will take place.

The state

Before defining our store, we will first have to focus on its state

Here is our state in its current form:

export interface AppState { todoItems: TodoItem[]; offset: number; pageSize: number;
}

We can divide its properties into two main categories:

  • Everything related to the pagination (offset, pageSize)
  • Everything related to the items themselves (only todoItems in our case)

From this, we can extract a subsequent interface, which has the responsibility of wrapping the parameters related to the pagination:

export interface PaginationDetails { offset: number; pageSize: number;
}

And another one which will wrap the content of the page:

export interface PageContent { todoItems: TodoItem[];
}

Using these two interfaces, we now have a state that is a bit more explicit:

export interface PaginatedItemsState { paginationDetails: PaginationDetails; pageContent: PageContent;
}

Great, let's move on!

The store itself

Setting the foundations

This store will be defined as an abstract class so that each subsequent store can add its own logic to is.

Using the previously defined state, we can write this:

@Injectable()
// 👇 Beware not to forget the `abstract` here
export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> { }

Creating selectors

Having state management is great, being able to access its content is better: let's add some selectors.

We can start by adding some base ones, to retrieve each property:

@Injectable()
export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState>
{ readonly selectPaginatedItemsState = this.select((state) => state); readonly selectPaginationDetails = this.select( this.selectPaginatedItemsState, ({ paginationDetails }) => paginationDetails ); readonly selectOffset = this.select( this.selectPaginationDetails, ({ offset }) => offset ); readonly selectPageSize = this.select( this.selectPaginationDetails, ({ pageSize }) => pageSize ); readonly selectPageContent = this.select( this.selectPaginatedItemsState, ({ pageContent }) => pageContent ); readonly selectTodoItems = this.select( this.selectPageContent, ({ todoItems }) => todoItems );
}

That's a lot of selectors but keep in mind that the purpose of this store is to be flexible enough so that any store that inherits from it can use those internals instead of rewriting them somewhere else

Handling pagination

From our store, let's add some updaters to update our state:

@Injectable()
export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState>
{ /* Selectors omitted here */ private readonly updatePagination = this.updater( (state, paginationDetails: PaginationDetails) => ({ ...state, paginationDetails }) ); private readonly updatePaginatedItems = this.updater( (state, pageContent: PageContent) => ({ ...state, pageContent }) );
}

I will be using one for the pagination and one for the paginated items but feel free to be more or less granular if you want to!

Finally, we can copy, paste and adapt a bit our two previous loadPage and loadNextPage effects from our AppComponentStore:

@Injectable()
export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState>
{ /* Selectors omitted here */ // 👇 Don't forget to also inject our service private readonly _todoItemService = inject(TodoItemService); readonly loadPage = this.effect((trigger$: Observable<void>) => { return trigger$.pipe( // 👇 We can directly access our pagination details from our selector withLatestFrom(this.selectPaginationDetails), switchMap(([, { offset, pageSize }]) => this._todoItemService.getTodoItems(offset, pageSize).pipe( tapResponse( (todoItems: TodoItem[]) => this.updatePaginatedItems({ todoItems }), () => console.error("Something went wrong") ) ) ) ); }); readonly loadNextPage = this.effect((trigger$: Observable<void>) => { return trigger$.pipe( // 👇 Same here withLatestFrom(this.selectPaginationDetails), tap(([, { offset, pageSize }]) => this.updatePagination({ offset: offset + pageSize, pageSize })), tap(() => this.loadPage()) ); }); /* Updaters omitted here */
}

Using NgRx lifecycle hooks

As we did in our first version, we can also take advantage of the OnStoreInit lifecycle hook here to load the page on startup:

@Injectable()
export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState> implements OnStoreInit
{ ngrxOnStoreInit() { this.loadPage(); }
}

By doing so, we can ensure that each store paginating items, and thus inheriting this one, will load the first page upon creation

Inheriting from our store

Finally, all that is left to do is for our AppComponentStore to inherit from our PaginatedItemsComponentStore instead of ComponentStore<AppState>

For that, we need to change our initial state and get rid of our own AppState to use the provided PaginatedItemsState:

// app.component-store.ts
const initialState: PaginatedItemsState = { paginationDetails: { offset: 0, pageSize: 10, }, pageContent: { todoItems: [], },
};

Once this is done, we can delete all updaters, effects and lifecycle hooks implementations from our component store and use instead the logic of the PaginatedComponentStore:

@Injectable()
export class AppComponentStore extends PaginatedItemsComponentStore
{ readonly vm$ = this.select( this.selectTodoItems, (todoItems) => ({ todoItems })); constructor() { super(initialState); }
}

If you run your application after this change, everything should still be working as it was before, yay!

Introducing generics

So far so good but let's not rejoice so fast

If everything as been as easy as copying and pasting code from our former store to the new one, it is only because we are constraining it to TodoItems

In a regular application, you may (surely) have more than one type of entity to paginate

Fortunately, TypeScript handles generics pretty well and this is something we can leverage to increase the reusability of our PaginatedItemsComponentStore

Rework our state

The first place to look at is our PageContent interface

In this one, we are defining the content as an array of TodoItem but we want to allow for a broader set of types

We may be tempted to change it to any[] but since we are using _Type_Script and not _Any_Script, we may find a better way

Using generics, we can specify to our interface that we would like to have an array of items whose type will be TItem since we don't know it yet:

export interface PageContent<TItem> { // 👇 Since we are manipulating items now I renamed the property items: TItem[];
}

By convention, I'm naming any generic type by starting with a T followed by its logical meaning

Updating this interface will need us to rewrite the PaginatedItemsState as well since we need to propagate the generics:

export interface PaginatedItemsState<TItem> { paginationDetails: PaginationDetails; pageContent: PageContent<TItem>;
}

Updating our store

With the updates made to the state, our store is no longer valid and we also need to propagate the generic type here

However, we don't want to use a concrete type yet or all our modifications would have been done for nothing

To address the fist compilation error, we will first need to also indicate our store that we will be using TItem:

@Injectable()
export abstract class PaginatedItemsComponentStore<TItem> extends ComponentStore<PaginatedItemsState<TItem>> implements OnStoreInit { /* ... */ }

After doing so, there is two small errors we need to address:

  • In our selectTodoItems selector, todoItems does no longer exists since we have rename it to items. We can fix it by changing the property name: 
 readonly selectItems = this.select( this.selectPageContent, ({ items }) => items );
  • In our updatePaginatedItems updated, PageContent is not aware of the generic type and we need to specify it: 
 private readonly updatePaginatedItems = this.updater( (state, pageContent: PageContent<TItem>) => ({ ...state, pageContent }) );

However, we now face a bigger issue: in the loadPage effect, we are calling the todoItemService and this service is very specific to our TodoItems

Delegate the fetching logic

From our PaginatedItemsComponentStore, there is no way for us to know in advance how a specific kind of TItem will be retrieved given an offset and the page size

However, a class that will know that is the implementing one

Fortunately, we are in an abstract class and we can let the child class define its own logic by adding an abstract method:

protected abstract getItems(paginationDetails: PaginationDetails): Observable<TItem[]>;

Using this method, we can now remove the instance of our service and replace its call by the abstract method:

- private readonly _todoItemService = inject(TodoItemService);
 readonly loadPage = this.effect((trigger$: Observable<void>) => { return trigger$.pipe( withLatestFrom(this.selectPaginationDetails), switchMap(([, { offset, pageSize }]) =>
- this._todoItemService.getTodoItems(offset, pageSize).pipe(
+ this.getItems({ offset, pageSize }).pipe(
 tapResponse(
- (todoItems: TodoItem[]) => this.updatePaginatedItems({ todoItems }),
+ (items: TItem[]) => this.updatePaginatedItems({ items }),
            () => console.error("Something went wrong")
          )
        )
      )
    );
  });

Updating the AppComponentStore

We're almost done! Now that our base store is generic, we need to specify in our AppComponentStore that TItem will be TodoItem for us

// 👇 Notice that we are now talking about `TodoItem`
const initialState: PaginatedItemsState<TodoItem> = { paginationDetails: { offset: 0, pageSize: 10, }, pageContent: { items: [], },
}; @Injectable()
export class AppComponentStore // 👇 Same here extends PaginatedItemsComponentStore<TodoItem>
{ readonly vm$ = this.select( // 👇 Don't forget that our selector has been renamed this.selectItems, (todoItems) => ({ todoItems }));
}

However, we now also need to implement that getItems method so that our parent component knows how to retrieve those TodoItems

For that, we will need to reinject a TodoItemService instance and call it from there:

 private readonly _todoItemService = inject(TodoItemService); protected getItems({ offset, pageSize }: PaginationDetails): Observable<TodoItem[]> { return this._todoItemService.getTodoItems(offset, pageSize); }

Building our app again, everything should still be working as before but, this time, paginating a new type of entity won't need you to rewrite the whole component store again!

In this article we saw how to take advantage of generics to lift the common pagination logic to an abstract component that we can later extend

If you would like to go a bit further you can try to:

  • Handle the loading and error logic
  • Add extra selectors (first item of the page, etc.)
  • Create a new PostService that is almost the same as the TodoItemService except that it is retrieving posts by calling https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts. You can then use this service to paginate Posts instead of TodoItems by defining a new component store inheriting from PaginatedItemsComponentStore<Post>

If you would like to check the resulting code, you can head on to the associated GitHub repository

I hope that you learnt something useful there and, as always, happy coding!

Items.Description:
欢迎

您好，欢迎阅读第 149 期 AWS 开源新闻通讯，这是地球上唯一一份每周为您提供 AWS 上最新鲜、最新的开源项目的新闻通讯。 我希望你们中的一些人能够在我们上一次基于开源构建的直播中看到这一集。 如果没有，您可以在此处观看重播。 本周我们有“mountpoint-s3”、“s3-access-for-squash”和“amazon-s3-tar-tool”等项目，它们提供了一些有用的工具来管理 S3 上的文件，“aws-serverless-ai” -stories”是讲故事的创意大师班，“earthquake-notifier”是一种无服务器解决方案，可让您时刻保持警觉并做好准备，等等！

我们还介绍了一些您最喜欢的开源项目，本周涵盖 Apache Cassandra、OpenSearch、Amazon EMR、Amazon Linux、Apache Airflow、Apache Hudi、Apache Iceberg、Nextcloud、Apache Kafka、Avalanche、AWS Parallel Cluster、Babelfish for Aurora PostgreSQL、还有很多！

反馈

请花 1 分钟时间完成这个简短的调查，并获得一些独家内容作为感谢。

庆祝开源贡献者

多亏了众多开源贡献者，本期通讯中分享的文章和项目才有可能实现。 我想大声喊出并感谢那些真正推动开源并使我们所有人都能在他们所创造的基础上学习和构建的人。

因此，感谢以下开源英雄：Alvaro Hernandez、Namrata H Shah、John "Preston" Mille、Dan Gross、Nenand Ilic、Alina Dima、Syed Rehan、Dr. Aparna Sundar、Helge Aufderheide、Herbert-John Kelly、Andrew Gargan , Ali Alemi, Houssem Chihoub, Sebastien Stormacq, and David Boyne

最新的开源项目

开源项目的好处在于您可以查看源代码。 如果您喜欢这些项目的外观，请务必查看代码，如果它对您有用，请与维护者联系以提供反馈、建议甚至提交贡献。

工具

安装点-s3

但是，需要注意的一件事是，Amazon S3 的 Mountpoint 目前是一个 alpha 版本，不应在生产中使用。 我喜欢阅读发布帖子， Amazon S3 的 Mountpoint 的内部故事，这是一个高性能的开源文件客户端， James Bornholt、Devabrat Kumar 和 Andy Warfield 向您展示了如何开始，以及您需要考虑的一些注意事项关于使用它之前，然后在幕后看看这个工具是如何开发的。

如果您发现自己要处理数千（或更多）小文件和 Amazon S3， s3-access-for-squash是一个很好的工具。 允许用户按需访问 S3 归档对象中压缩文件的工具。 如果我们以大量小型文本文件存档用例为例，您可能有一个大型本地源代码树想要存档到 S3。 如果您要直接向/从 S3 注入/提取数万个文件，这将是低效且成本高昂的。 使用此工具，您可以在上传到 S3 之前将小文件压缩并聚合到单个大存档对象中。 然后，当稍后需要访问单个文件时，您可以通过 S3 GetObject API 获取，该 API 在 S3 Object Lambda 函数后面将自动定位 S3 中存档对象内的文件内容，并将唯一需要的字节提取回用户。 确实很酷。

请务必查看 README 中的通知，因为它确实提供了有关如何/何时使用它的额外指导：

大量小文件存档用例的 S3 Squash Archive 目标，通常一旦注入就很少访问。 如果您的用例需要高频访问完整数据集，请按正常方式将您的文件上传到 S3 对象，通过将此文件执行到 S3 对象 1x1 映射，您可以从 S3 获得最大吞吐量，这是最具成本效益的方式。

此 cli 工具利用现有的 Amazon S3 API 在 Amazon S3 上创建档案，以后可以将其转移到任何冷存储层。 生成的文件遵循 tar 文件格式，可以使用标准的 tar 工具提取。

确保您阅读了 README，因为它包含有关它在后台如何工作的更多详细信息、有关定价注意事项的一些详细信息，以及您应该注意的一些限制。

我真的很喜欢这背后的想法，如果你一直在社交媒体上关注大卫，你就会看到这个项目是如何发展的。 该项目是事件驱动应用程序的一个示例，它使用 Lambda、EventBridge、DynamoDB、App Runner、ChatGPT 和 DALL-E 为您的孩子每晚生成一个新的睡前故事。

我很高兴地说，除了代码，他还整理了一篇博文，使用 ChatGPT 和 DALL-E 实现事件驱动的无服务器故事生成应用程序，指导您自己实现这个。 本周必读。

地震通知器

在引擎盖下，它使用国家地球物理和火山学公共 API (INGV) 并每小时扫描一次。

亚马逊-keyspaces-python-util-脚本

我经常发现这些为研讨会和教程放在一起的脚本非常有用，因此出于这个原因分享这个项目。

演示、示例、解决方案和研讨会

多仿真作业-aws-robomaker-demo

该演示设置了一个 Docker 容器格式 Talker 和 Listener 以通过 Fast DDS Discovery Server 进行 TCP 通信。 源代码提供了一个设置模板，当您想要使用 AWS RoboMaker 同时与多个模拟作业进行通信和协作时，可以使用该模板。

亚马逊 Linux 2023

作为 Amazon Linux 发行版一段时间以来的大用户，我很高兴我们现在推出了下一个版本 Amazon Linux 2023。在 Amazon Linux 2023，具有长期支持的云优化 Linux 发行版一文中，我的同事Sebastien Stormacq 对某些功能进行了很好的概述，重点是安全性和长期支持计划。

阿帕奇冰山

在博文中，使用 Apache Iceberg、Amazon EMR Serverless 和 Amazon Athena 构建无服务器事务数据湖，Houssem Chihoub 提供了有关如何使用 Apache Iceberg 在 Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) 上构建无服务器事务数据湖的实践指南) 使用 Amazon EMR 无服务器和 Amazon Athena [动手]

阿帕奇卡夫卡

关于如何运行 Apache Kafka，您有很多选择。 在这篇如何为您选择合适的 Amazon MSK 集群类型的博文中，Ali Alemi 向您展示了他的流程，该流程可以帮助您从应用程序的要求和可用资源逆向工作，找到适合您的选择。

库伯内斯

在 Kubernetes 世界中花费了越来越多的时间后，我发现下一篇文章非常有帮助。 未来云

继该项目的上一篇文章之后，Helge Aufderheide 这次写道， Advanced Nextcloud Workflows with Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3)基于该想法构建并展示了高级功能和优化，例如在您上传文件时启动工作流和优化您的 Amazon S3 使用成本。 其他帖子和快速阅读

实例探究

快速更新

打开搜索

我鼓励大家看看这篇文章。

另外，您是否注册了 OpenSearch 时事通讯？ 查看最新版本的OpenSearch Project Newsletter - 第 1 卷，第 2 期，并确保添加书签/订阅以了解最新的 OpenSearch 新闻。

阿帕奇卡桑德拉

客户端时间戳是 Cassandra 兼容的时间戳，为表中的每个单元格保留。 通过让应用程序确定写入顺序，您可以使用客户端时间戳来解决单元级冲突。 例如，当多个应用程序对同一数据进行更新或写入操作由于可变网络延迟而乱序到达时，Amazon Keyspaces 使用这些时间戳根据行内各个单元格的写入时间戳来处理写入。 要使用客户端时间图，请在数据操作语言 (DML) CQL 查询中使用 USING TIMESTAMP 子句。 通过此次发布，您还可以使用 WRITETIME 函数查看使用客户端时间戳为表中特定单元格存储的时间戳值。

使用委派管理员，您的主账户可以向您组织中的另一个账户授予访问权限，以跟踪 AWS 上的 Linux 订阅。 委派管理员使您可以灵活地将订阅管理与计费和其他中央管理任务分开。

您可以在您的中央付款人或管理账户中配置 AWS License Manager，并选择另一个 AWS 账户作为委派管理员。 配置委派管理员账户后，您可以跨区域和账户发现 Linux 订阅，并执行监管任务，例如查看 CloudWatch 指标或设置警报。

亚马逊电子病历

在通过托管端点运行交互式 Spark 工作负载时，您现在可以获得客户级别的指标。 直到现在，运行托管端点的客户还没有一种机制来监控或可视化他们基于内核的执行行为。 如果无法了解故障、延迟或成功启动等指标，客户可能会遇到故障排除和了解托管端点发生的情况的困难。 在此版本中，客户将能够通过 Amazon CloudWatch 对内核生命周期操作（例如请求计数、请求延迟、请求错误和内核启动失败）利用指标，从而监控、创建警报并更好地解决其托管端点中的问题。

第一天

第二天

动态53

在Build on Open Source 的前一集中，AWS 精英 Alvaro Hernandez 与我们分享了他正在使用的一个新项目 Dyna53。 这个项目允许你使用 Route53 作为数据库！ 如果这引起了你的注意，那太好了，现在通过观看主持人 Namrata H Shah 与 Alvaro 的谈话来了解更多信息。

这个来自 Mend 可爱的人的简短教程向您展示了如何与源代码控制集成并快速检测开源工件及其已知漏洞和许可风险。 以开源为基础

如果您错过了 Build on Open Source 的最新一集，第 3 集查看了这个和之前的时事通讯，并邀请了特邀嘉宾 AWS 社区建设者 John“Preston”Mille 谈论他的项目 ecs-compose-x。 如果你错过了，别担心，你可以在这里重播

对于那些不熟悉这个节目的人，Build on Open Source 是我们回顾这个时事通讯的地方，然后邀请特邀嘉宾深入研究他们的开源项目。 期待大量的代码、演示和希望的笑声。 我们整理了一个播放列表，以便您可以轻松访问 Build on Open Source 节目的所有（八）集。 基于开源播放列表

如果您计划在 2023 年举办任何活动，无论是虚拟活动、面对面活动还是混合活动，请与我们联系，因为我很乐意与读者分享您活动的详细信息。

使用 AWS Proton 和 Backstage 为开发人员赋能
如果您能加入利物浦 AWS 用户组，那么这将是一场盛会。 了解更多信息并在此处注册

以开源为基础

原文:

Welcome

Hello and welcome to edition #149 of the AWS open source newsletter, the only newsletter on the planet that serves you up a weekly dose of the freshest, latest open source projects on AWS. I hope some of you were able to catch this episode reviewed on our last Build on Open Source livestream. If not, you can catch the replay here. This week we have projects such "mountpoint-s3", "s3-access-for-squash", and "amazon-s3-tar-tool" which provide some useful tools for managing your files on S3, "aws-serverless-ai-stories" a creative masterclass in storytelling, "earthquake-notifier" a serverless solution to keep you alerted and ready, and more!

We also feature some of your favourite open source projects, this week covering Apache Cassandra, OpenSearch, Amazon EMR, Amazon Linux, Apache Airflow, Apache Hudi, Apache Iceberg, Nextcloud, Apache Kafka, Avalanche, AWS Parallel Cluster, Babelfish for Aurora PostgreSQL, and many more!

Feedback

Please please please take 1 minute to complete this short survey and get some exclusive content as a thank you.

Celebrating open source contributors

The articles and projects shared in this newsletter are only possible thanks to the many contributors in open source. I would like to shout out and thank those folks who really do power open source and enable us all to learn and build on top of what they have created.

So thank you to the following open source heroes: Alvaro Hernandez, Namrata H Shah, John "Preston" Mille, Dan Gross, Nenand Ilic, Alina Dima, Syed Rehan, Dr. Aparna Sundar, Helge Aufderheide, Herbert-John Kelly, Andrew Gargan, Ali Alemi, Houssem Chihoub, Sebastien Stormacq, and David Boyne

Latest open source projects

The great thing about open source projects is that you can review the source code. If you like the look of these projects, make sure you that take a look at the code, and if it is useful to you, get in touch with the maintainer to provide feedback, suggestions or even submit a contribution.

Tools

mountpoint-s3

mountpoint-s3 was the big news last week, a new project that provides a simple, high-throughput file client for mounting an Amazon S3 bucket as a local file system. One thing to note however, is Mountpoint for Amazon S3 is currently an alpha release and should not be used in production. I enjoyed reading the launch post, The inside story on Mountpoint for Amazon S3, a high-performance open source file client where James Bornholt, Devabrat Kumar, and Andy Warfield show you how you can get started, some of the considerations you need to think about before using it, and then a look behind the scenes at how this tool was developed.

s3-access-for-squash

s3-access-for-squash is a great tool if you find yourself working with thousands (or more) small files and Amazon S3. Tools to allow user access files squashed in S3 archive object on demand. If we take an example of a massive small text files archive use case, it is likely you have a large local source code tree wants to be archived to S3. It will be inefficient and costly if you were to inject/ingest tens of thousands files direct to/from S3. With this tool, you can compress and aggregate small files into a single big archive object before upload to S3. Then, when a individual file needs to be accessed later, you can fetch through S3 GetObject API which in behind a S3 Object Lambda function will automatically locate file contents inside of archive object in S3 and extract the only necessary bytes back to user. Very cool indeed.

Make sure you check the notice in the README though, as it does provide additional guidance on how/when to use this:

S3 Squash Archive target for massive small files archive use case, typically once injected seldom access. if your use case need high frequency access full dataset, please upload your file to S3 Object as normal way, by doing this file to S3 object 1x1 mapping, you can get MAX throughput from S3 and it's most cost effective way.

amazon-s3-tar-tool

amazon-s3-tar-tool or s3tar, is utility tool to create a tarball of existing objects in Amazon S3. s3tar allows customers to group existing Amazon S3 objects into TAR files without having to download the files. This cli tool leverages existing Amazon S3 APIs to create the archives on Amazon S3 that can be later transitioned to any of the cold storage tiers. The files generated follow the tar file format and can be extracted with standard tar tools.

Make sure you read the README as it contains more details around how it works under the hood, some details around pricing considerations, and then some limitations you should be aware of.

aws-serverless-ai-stories

aws-serverless-ai-stories this a new project from AWS Serverless Advocate (and open source bar raiser) David Boyne. I really love the idea behind this, and if you have been following David on social media, you will have seen how this project developed. This project is an example of an event-driven application that generates a new bed time story for your children every night using Lambda, EventBridge, DynamoDB, App Runner, ChatGPT and DALL-E.

I am happy to say that along with the code, he has put together a blog post, Implementing an event-driven serverless story generation application with ChatGPT and DALL-E, that guides you through implementing this for yourself. This weeks essential read.

earthquake-notifier

earthquake-notifier is an example serverless solution based on Lambda (in Rust), EventBridge and SNS to monitor for earthquakes and send email notifications. Under the hood, it uses the National Institute of Geophysics and Volcanology public APIs (INGV) and scans them every hour.

amazon-keyspaces-python-util-scripts

amazon-keyspaces-python-util-scripts if you are looking to investigate Amazon Keyspaces for Apache Cassandra, then these scripts accompany a tutorial (Scalability and familiarity with Amazon Keyspaces )that will help you gain familiarity with this service. I often find that these scripts that are put together for workshops and tutorials are super helpful, so sharing this project for that reason.

Demos, Samples, Solutions and Workshops

multi-simulation-jobs-aws-robomaker-demo

multi-simulation-jobs-aws-robomaker-demo is a demo that shows how you can enable communication between ROS2 applications deployed in AWS RoboMaker's multiple simulation jobs. This demo sets up a Docker container format Talker and Listener to do TCP communication through Fast DDS Discovery Server. The source code provides a setting template that can be used when you want to communicate and work with multiple simulation jobs simultaneously using AWS RoboMaker.

AWS and Community blog posts

Amazon Linux 2023

As a big user of Amazon Linux distributions for a while, I am happy that we have now launched the next version, Amazon Linux 2023. In the post, Amazon Linux 2023, a Cloud-Optimized Linux Distribution with Long-Term Support, my colleague Sebastien Stormacq provides a great overview of some of the features, with a focus on security and long term support planning.

Apache Iceberg

In the post, Build a serverless transactional data lake with Apache Iceberg, Amazon EMR Serverless, and Amazon Athena, Houssem Chihoub provides a hands on guide on how you can build a serverless transactional data lake with Apache Iceberg on Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) using Amazon EMR Serverless and Amazon Athena [hands on]

Apache Kafka

There are many options you have when it comes to how you run your Apache Kafka. In this post, How to choose the right Amazon MSK cluster type for you, Ali Alemi shows you his process that may help you work backward from your application’s requirements and the resources available to you, to find the right choice for you.

Kubernetes

Having spent an increasing amount of time immersed in the world of Kubernetes, I found this next post very helpful. Herbert-John Kelly and Andrew Gargan have penned Automate prework to save time deploying an Amazon EKS Kubernetes cluster, that shows you how you can automate a lot of the configuration work you need to do when setting up your Kubernetes clusters - in this particular case, using CloudFormation. [hands on]

Nextcloud

Nextcloud is an open source platform to store, share and manage files over the web. Following on from a previous post on this project, Helge Aufderheide this time writes, Advanced Nextcloud Workflows with Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) that build on that idea and demonstrate advanced functionalities and optimisations, such as starting workflows when you upload a file and optimising your Amazon S3 usage for cost. [hands on]

Other posts and quick reads

Case Studies

Quick updates

OpenSearch

The OpenSearch community survey reports were published last week, and Dr. Aparna Sundar put together a post, OpenSearch Project Q1 community survey results, that walked through the highlights. I would encourage you all to check this post out.

Also, have you signed up for the OpenSearch newsletter? Check the latest edition, OpenSearch Project Newsletter - Volume 1, Issue 2 and make sure you bookmark/subscribe to keep up to date with the latest OpenSearch news.

Apache Cassandra

Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra) is a scalable, serverless, highly available, and fully managed Apache Cassandra-compatible database service. Amazon Keyspaces now supports client-side timestamps. Client-side timestamps are Cassandra-compatible timestamps that are persisted for each cell in your table. You can use client-side timestamps for cell level conflict resolution by letting applications determine the order of writes. For example, when multiple applications make updates to the same data or when write operations arrive out of order due to variable network latency, Amazon Keyspaces uses these timestamps to process the writes based on the write timestamps of individual cells within rows. To use client-side timestmaps, use the USING TIMESTAMP clause in your Data Manipulation Language (DML) CQL query. With this launch, you can also use the WRITETIME function to see the timestamp value that is stored for a specific cell on tables using client-side timestamps.

Linux

AWS License Manager extends support for delegated administrator to Linux subscriptions discovery and governance capabilities. Using delegated administrator, your management account can give access to another account in your organisation to track Linux subscriptions on AWS. Delegated administrator gives you the flexibility to separate subscription management from billing and other central management tasks.

You can configure AWS License Manager in your central payer or management account and pick another AWS account as the delegated administrator. Once the delegated administrator account is configured, you can discover Linux subscriptions across Regions and accounts, and perform governance tasks such as viewing CloudWatch metrics or setting up alarms.

Amazon EMR

You can now get customer-level metrics when running interactive Spark workloads via managed endpoints. Amazon EMR on EKS enables customers to run open-source big data frameworks such as Apache Spark on Amazon EKS. Amazon EMR on EKS customers can setup and use a managed endpoint (available in preview) to run interactive workloads using integrated development environments (IDEs) such as EMR Studio.

Until now, customers running managed endpoints did not have a mechanism to monitor or visualise kernel-based execution behaviour for them. Without visibility into metrics such as failures, latency or successful launches, customers might experience difficulty troubleshooting and understanding what is happening with a managed endpoint. With this release, customers will be able to monitor, create alarms and better troubleshoot issues in their managed endpoints, by leveraging metrics via Amazon CloudWatch for kernel lifecycle operations such as request counts, request latency, request errors and kernel launch failures.

Videos of the week

IoT Builders Live from Embedded World 2023

The IoT team streamed live from Embedded World 2023 last week, and if you were not able to view live, then you can watch them on replay.

Day Two

In a previous episode of Build on Open Source, AWS Hero Alvaro Hernandez shared with us a new project he was working with, Dyna53. This project allows you to use Route53 as a database! If that got your attention, great, now find out more by watching your host Namrata H Shah speak with Alvaro.

MEND and CodeCatalyst

This short tutorial from the lovely folk at Mend show you how you can integrate with your source control and quickly detect open source artefacts and their known vulnerabilities and licensing risks. Mend also provides all the information you need to fix these artefacts automatically.

Build on Open Source

If you missed the latest episode of Build on Open Source, Episode 3 looked at this and the previous newsletter, and had special guest, AWS Community Builder John "Preston" Mille to talk about his project, ecs-compose-x. If you missed it, don't worry you can catch it on replay here

Items.Title: 为什么每个开发人员（初学者、中级和专家）都应该开始写博客。 | Why Every Developer (Beginner, intermediate and Expert) Should Start Blogging.
Items.Description:
博客已成为开发人员表达自己的一种流行方式。 作为开发人员，您可能将大部分时间花在编写代码、调试以及与团队协作上。 有这么多事情要做，您可能没有考虑过创建博客。 但是，博客可以成为开发人员提高技能的宝贵工具，尤其是对于初学者，可以分享他们的知识和经验，建立他们的品牌，并与他们所在领域的其他人联系。

人们开始写博客的原因有很多，这可能是为了建立个人品牌、与他人交流或创建作品集。 上述理由是开始写博客的很好的理由，但是如果你有理由（为什么）开始写博客，这将非常有帮助，这将有助于控制你并保持你的动力。 我将在下面揭开我开始写博客的原因。

为什么我开始写博客。

对于成长：多年来，我发现帮助别人理解一门课程也能帮助我更好地理解它。 它有助于激励我学习更多，因为我喜欢并热衷于帮助他人。

对于表达：博客帮助我在思想上变得更具表现力，然后将它们写下来。 在我开始写任何文章之前，我通常会在脑海中思考几个小时，尤其是在第二天晚上我会把它写下来。

对于我的消费：这听起来可能很奇怪，但我是为自己写的，而且我阅读我的博客文章的次数比我的读者多。 大多数时候，我在做家务时使用音频博客功能来收听过去几周写的主题，这有助于我改进下一篇文章，并在我的博客中更多地做我自己，以便我的观众可以与我的个性，因为我不想听起来很聪明但很真实。

对于问责制：写下我学到的东西有助于让我走上正轨，例如，我可能想开始学习另一种编程语言而不完全理解我目前正在学习的语言，因为我记录了我的旅程并与我的观众分享了其中一些看起来我来决定。 我不想让他们失望，所以我自然会保持在正轨上。

提高自己：我让我的读者纠正了我在以前文章中犯的一些错误，我喜欢被纠正，因为我通过它们得到了显着的成长。 我也让我的读者为我写的一个主题做出贡献，我从他们身上学到了很多东西。

树立自己的权威

通过博客文章分享您的知识和经验，您可以确立自己在该领域的权威地位。 这可以帮助您获得其他开发人员以及潜在客户和雇主的认可和尊重。

联网

博客是与其他与您有共同兴趣和爱好的开发人员联系的好方法。 您可以使用您的博客来分享您的想法和想法，并与社区中的其他开发人员进行讨论。

探索和学习新事物

撰写博文需要研究和学习，这可以帮助您扩展知识和技能。 通过撰写有关新技术或工具的文章，您还可以加深对这些主题的理解，并在您的领域变得更加博学。

建立你的个人品牌

博客可以帮助您建立个人品牌并使您从其他开发人员中脱颖而出。 通过分享您独特的观点和声音，您可以在您的行业中成为与众不同且令人难忘的人物。

获得新客户或工作机会

博客还可以帮助您获得新客户或工作机会。 通过展示您的技能和知识，您可以向潜在客户或雇主展示您的价值。 您的博客也可以作为您的作品集，这可以帮助您从其他候选人中脱颖而出。

提高你的写作和沟通技巧

无论您是与团队成员交流还是编写代码文档，写作都是开发人员的一项基本技能。 通过定期写博客，您可以提高写作技巧并成为更好的沟通者。

记录你的进步

博客还可以记录您作为开发人员的进步。 您可以记录您的旅程，分享您的成功和失败，并反思您一路上学到的东西。

初学者应该从哪里开始写博客。

开发人员可以使用几个免费网站开始写博客。 这里有几个选项：

它是免费使用的。 您可以撰写各种主题，包括技术和编程。 我个人将它用于我的博客文章。

您可以免费注册，并开始撰写与软件开发相关的文章。

您可以使用该平台创建博客、作品集或个人网站。 您还可以使用各种框架（例如 Jekyll 或 Hugo）来创建您的网站。

您可以从各种模板中进行选择，并使用插件和小部件自定义您的网站。 它易于使用，您可以立即开始撰写博客文章。

您可以免费创建博客，并使用各种模板自定义您的网站。 您还可以将您的博客连接到您的 Google 帐户，以便轻松管理您的博客和在社交媒体上分享您的帖子。

我还使用这个网站，因为它是在自定义域上启动开发人员博客的最简单方法之一。

这些只是免费网站的几个选项，可以让开发人员开始写博客。 每个平台都有其独特的功能和优势，因此值得探索它们以找到适合您的平台。

结论：

找到您的原因，然后开始您自己的博客。 博客对开发人员来说是一个有价值的工具，它可以帮助您确立自己的权威地位、与其他开发人员联系、学习新事物、建立您的个人品牌、获得新客户或工作机会、提高您的写作和沟通技巧，并记录你的进步。 无论您是刚刚起步还是经验丰富的开发人员，博客都可以帮助您实现目标并推动您的职业发展。

感谢您通读，如果您喜欢这篇文章，请考虑在 (Twitter) mobile.twitter.com/Simply_RiTq?first_ru上与我联系。

干杯！！！



原文:

Blogging has become a popular way in which Developers express themselves. As a developer, you probably spend most of your time writing code, debugging, and collaborating with your team. With so much to do, you may not have considered starting a blog. However, blogging can be a valuable tool for developers to improve their skills, especially for beginners, share their knowledge and experience, build their brand, and connect with others in their field.

There are so many reasons why people start blogging, this may be to build a personal brand, Communicate with others, or Create a portfolio. The above reasons are very good reasons to start blogging, but it would be quite helpful to have your reasons (Why) to start blogging this will help keep you in check and keep you motivated. I will be unveiling the reasons why I started blogging below.

Why I Started blogging.

For Growth: Over the years, I have found out that helping others understand a course also helps me understand it much better. It helps motivate me to learn more since I enjoy and have a passion for helping others.

For Expression: Blogging has helped me a lot to become more expressive in my thoughts and then put them in writing. Before I start writing any article, I normally think it through in my mind for some hours, most especially throughout the night for the next day I would write it down.

For My Consumption: This might sound weird, but I write for myself, and I read my blog post more than my audience. Most of the time, I use the audio blog feature to listen to topics I have written in the past weeks while doing house chores, this helps me improve on my next post, and be myself more in my blog so that my audience can connect with my personality since I don't want to sound smart but authentic.

For Accountability: Writing what I have learned helps keep me on track, for instance, I might want to start learning another programming language without fully understanding the one I'm currently learning since I document my journey and share with my audience some of which look up to me. I Wouldn't want to disappoint them so I would naturally stay on track.

To Improve Myself: I have had my readers correct some mistakes I have made in my previous articles, I like to be corrected because I grow significantly through them. I have also had my readers contribute to a topic I have written, and I have learned from them all.

(Generally) Why Every Developer Should Start Blogging.

Establish yourself as an authority

By sharing your knowledge and experience through blog posts, you can establish yourself as an authority in your field. This can help you gain recognition and respect from other developers, as well as potential clients and employers.

Networking

Blogging is a great way to connect with other developers who share your interests and passions. You can use your blog to share your thoughts and ideas and engage in discussions with other developers in your community.

Explore and Learn new things

Writing blog posts requires research and learning, which can help you expand your knowledge and skills. By writing about new technologies or tools, you can also deepen your understanding of these topics and become more knowledgeable in your field.

Build your personal brand

Blogging can help you build your personal brand and differentiate yourself from other developers. By sharing your unique perspective and voice, you can establish yourself as a distinctive and memorable figure in your industry.

Land new clients or job opportunities

Blogging can also help you land new clients or job opportunities. By showcasing your skills and knowledge, you can demonstrate your value to potential clients or employers. Your blog can also serve as a portfolio of your work, which can help you stand out from other candidates.

Improve your writing and Communication skills

Writing is an essential skill for developers, whether you're communicating with team members or documenting your code. By blogging regularly, you can improve your writing skills and become a better communicator.

Keep a record of your progress

Blogging can also serve as a record of your progress as a developer. You can document your journey, share your successes and failures, and reflect on what you've learned along the way.

Where You Should Start Blogging as a Beginner.

There are several free websites that developers can use to start blogging. Here are a few options:

Medium - Medium is a popular platform for bloggers, with a focus on quality writing and storytelling. It's free to use. You can write on a variety of topics, including technology and programming. I personally use it for my blog post.

Dev.to - Dev.to is a community of developers that provides a platform for sharing knowledge and connecting with other developers. You can sign up for free, and start writing articles on topics related to software development.

GitHub Pages - GitHub Pages allow developers to create a website for free using their GitHub account. You can use the platform to create a blog, portfolio, or personal website. You can also use a variety of frameworks, such as Jekyll or Hugo, to create your website.

WordPress.com - WordPress.com is a free platform that allows you to create a blog or website. You can choose from a variety of templates and customize your site using plugins and widgets. It's easy to use, and you can start writing blog posts right away.

Blogger - Blogger is a free blogging platform owned by Google. You can create a blog for free, and customize your site using a variety of templates. You can also connect your blog to your Google account, making it easy to manage your blog and share your posts on social media.

Hasnode.com- This is also a free blogging platform for bloggers. I also use this website because it's one of the easiest ways to start a developer blog on a custom domain.

These are just a few options for free websites to start blogging as a developer. Each platform has its own unique features and benefits, so it's worth exploring them to find the one that's right for you.

Conclusion:

Find your WHY, and start your own blog. Blogging is a valuable tool for developers that can help you establish yourself as an authority, connect with other developers, learn new things, build your personal brand, land new clients or job opportunities, improve your writing and communication skills, and keep a record of your progress. Whether you're just starting out or you're an experienced developer, blogging can help you achieve your goals and advance your career.

Thank you for reading through, if you liked this article consider connecting with me on (Twitter) mobile.twitter.com/Simply_RiTq?first_ru….

Cheers!!!

Items.Title: 使用 AWS CI/CD 工具在 S3 中部署 NextJs 应用程序 | Deployment of NextJs app in S3 using AWS CI/CD tools
Items.Description:
您好，在本文中，我将向您介绍使用 AWS CI/CD 工具、 CodeCommit和CodePipeline部署 Next.js 应用程序的过程。 在我们开始之前，请确保您拥有 AWS 账户并熟悉 AWS 和 Git 流程的基本概念。

第 1 步：创建 Next.js 应用程序

首先，让我们使用命令行创建一个新的 Next.js 应用程序。 您可以使用标志 --typescript 选择使用 TypeScript 创建它。

 yarn create next-app my-next-app cd my-next-app

第 2 步：创建CodeCommit存储库

现在我们已经设置了应用程序，让我们在 AWS CodeCommit中创建一个新的存储库来存储我们的代码。 为此，请转到 AWS CodeCommit控制台并单击“创建存储库”按钮。
为此，您可以按照图像中显示的给定步骤进行操作。

单击Create repository按钮，您将看到以下控制台。 在这里你需要写下你的 repo 的名称并根据需要添加描述（这是可选的）。 之后，点击Create button 。

创建 repo 后，您将在控制台中看到以下说明。 请完成每个步骤_（个人推荐）_。 在步骤 3 部分下，您将找到用于克隆此存储库的 git 命令。

现在要克隆此存储库，您必须能够创建 Git 凭据。 您可以按照官方文档中显示的这些步骤进行操作。

将此 git origin 添加到您现有的代码库中。 如果您不熟悉此步骤，请阅读此官方文档。

第 3 步：创建用于部署的CodePipeline

接下来，我们需要在 AWS CodePipeline中创建一个新的CodePipeline来处理我们应用程序的部署。 完成这些步骤。

在 AWS CodePipeline控制台中，单击“创建管道”并按照下图中所示的步骤进行操作，然后单击“下一步”。 您可以在此处选择New service role ，AWS 将为您创建一个新角色。 您需要稍后编辑此角色，这将在下面的第 5 步中进行描述。

在下一步中添加源提供程序。 我们应用程序的源提供者（代码容器）是 AWS CodeCommit ，因此请选择一个。 在这里，分别选择存储库名称和分支名称（主分支通常是“master”，或者可以是“develop”、“main”等），并将所有其他配置保留为默认值，然后单击“下一步”。

完成第二步后，第三步非常重要。 在这里您将选择构建提供者。 您可以选择任何一个，但在这里我坚持使用AWS CodeBuild 。 选择此选项后，您将看到其他配置选项，您需要保留默认设置，只需要创建一个项目名称。

创建项目时，保留所有默认值。 在环境部分，对于操作系统，选择Amazon Linux 2并选择standard运行时。


在Buildspec部分，您可以为构建 yml 文件指定任何自定义名称。 现在选择默认的buildspec.yml并保留所有其他配置，然后点击Continue to CodePipeline 。

现在，您将被重定向到上一步，控制台将显示您的项目。 现在点击下一步按钮。

在此步骤中，您必须指定部署提供程序。 现在选择 S3 并提及 Bucket 名称并选中Extract file before deploy复选框，如图所示，然后点击 Next。

现在检查所有设置并创建管道。 您可能会在构建过程失败的地方看到部署的自动进度。 所以，不要惊慌，我们还有两个配置要完成。

第 4 步：创建 IAM 策略

因为我们的管道需要访问 **S3 ** 和 **CloudFront ** 服务。 因此，通过单击以下链接转到部署部分以查看所有日志，如图所示。

现在，在构建详细信息选项卡下，您将看到服务角色（在命名管道时在步骤 3 中创建）。 单击该角色，您将被重定向到 IAM 控制台。

现在附加内联策略，如下图所示，用于S3写入 (PutObject) 和CloudFront写入 (CreateInvalidation)

转到创建策略控制台后，请按照以下步骤操作。

  1. 首先选择服务s3 。
  2. 在 Actions 部分下，展开写入框并选择PutObject 。
  3. 提及 S3 存储桶和对象。
  4. 再次点击Add additional permissions部分并选择服务CloudFront
  5. 在 Actions 下，展开写入框并选择CreateInvalidation 。
  6. 创建策略。

第五步：在vs代码中创建buildspec.yml文件

现在你需要在根目录_（你的项目 src 目录所在的位置）_ 在 vs 代码中创建buildspec.yml文件。 创建文件并粘贴给定的地图。

 version: 0.1 phases: pre_build: commands: - yarn install build: commands: - yarn run build post_build: commands: - aws s3 cp --recursive public-read ./build s3://<bucketname>/ - aws s3 cp public-read --cache-control="max-age=0, no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate" ./build/index.html s3://<bucketname>/ - aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id <your-cloudfront-id> --paths /index.html artifacts: files: - 'build/*' - 'build/**/*'

上面描述的 YAML 文件非常简单。

  • 在pre_build阶段，脚本会安装所有必需的 NPM 包依赖项。

  • 在构建阶段，脚本运行yarn build命令来编译 Web 应用并将其捆绑到构建目录中。

  • 在post_build阶段，脚本执行三个操作。 首先，它将构建目录的所有内容复制到指定的 **S3 ** 存储桶，并使它们可公开访问。 接下来，它再次复制index.html文件并设置特定的缓存 HTTP 标头以防止浏览器缓存该文件。 最后，它创建一个“失效”，指示 **CloudFront ** 分发从 S3 源存储桶刷新其内容。

第六步：将代码推送到源站

至此，我们现在已经完成了需要在 AWS 中完成的所有配置，以便CodeBuild将我们的NextJs应用程序部署到S3 。 因此，提交更改并将分支推送到存储库。 单击将显示部署进度时，您将看到progress标签，几分钟后您的代码将部署到 S3。 完整的过程看起来像这样。

希望您今天喜欢这篇文章并学到了新技能。 如果你想从我这里得到更多，那么不要忘记关注。 另外，如果您有任何疑问或反馈，请发表评论。 谢谢你，部署愉快！



原文:

Hey there, in this article, I am going to walk you through the process of deploying a Next.js app using AWS CI/CD tools, CodeCommitand CodePipeline. Before we begin, make sure you have an AWS account and are familiar with the basic concepts of AWS and Git flow.

Step 1: Create a Next.js app

First, let's create a new Next.js app using the command line. You can choose to create it with TypeScript by using the flag --typescript.

yarn create next-app my-next-app
cd my-next-app

Step 2: Create a CodeCommitrepository

Now that we have our app set up, let's create a new repository in AWS CodeCommitto store our code. To do this, go to the AWS CodeCommitconsole and click on the "Create repository" button.
For this you can follow given steps shown in images.

Click on Create repository button you will see the following console. Here you need to write a name of your repo and add description if you want (It is optional). After that, hit Create button.

After creating repo, you will see the following instructions in the console. Please go through the each steps_(personal recommendation)_. Under the step 3 section, you will find the git command to clone this repo.

Now for cloning this repo, you must be able to create the Git credentials. You can follow this steps shown in official documentation.

Add this git origin to your existing code base. If you are not familiar with this steps go through this official documentation.

Step 3:Create a CodePipelinefor deployment

Next, we need to create a new CodePipelinein AWS CodePipelineto handle the deployment of our app. Go through this steps.

In the AWS CodePipelineconsole, click on "Create pipeline" and follow the steps as shown in the images below and hit Next. Here you can select the New service role and AWS will create a new role for you. You need to edit this role later which is described in the step 5 below.

In the Next step add the source provider. The source provider( code container) for our app is AWS CodeCommitso choose the one. Here, choose the repository name and the branch name(main branch usually "master" or could be any like "develop", "main" etc) respectively and leave all other configuration as default and hit Next.

Once you are done with the second step, third one is pretty important one. Here you will going to choose the build provider. You can choose any one but here I am sticking with the AWS CodeBuild. Once you select this option, you will see other configuration options where you need to leave as default and only need to create a project name.

while creating the project, leave everything default. And In the environment section, for Operating System, choose Amazon Linux 2 and choose standard Runtime.


In the Buildspec section you can give any custom name for the build yml file. For now choose buildspec.yml which is the default one and leave all other configuration as it is and hit Continue to CodePipeline.

Now, you will be redirect to previous step and console will show your project. Now hit Next button.

In this step you have to specify the deploy provider. Choose S3 for now and mention Bucket name and check the Extract file before deploy checkbox as shown and hit Next.

Now review all the settings and create the pipeline. You may see automatic progress of the deployment where your build process will fail. So, don't get panic, we still have two more configuration to complete.

Step 4: create the IAM policy

As our pipeline needs access to the **S3 **and **CloudFront **services. So, Go to the deployment section to see all the logs by clicking the following link as shown in image.

Now under the Build details tab you will see Service role (created at Step 3 while naming the pipeline). Click in the role and you will be redirect to the IAM console.

Now Attach the inline policies as shown in the figure below for S3 write (PutObject) and CloudFront write (CreateInvalidation)

After going to the Create Policy console follow this steps.

  1. First choose the service s3.
  2. Under Actions section, expand write box and choose PutObject.
  3. Mention S3 bucket and object.
  4. Again click on Add additional permissions section and choose service CloudFront
  5. Under Actions, expand write box and choose CreateInvalidation.
  6. create the policy.

Step 5: create a buildspec.yml file in the vs code

Now you need to create buildspec.yml file in the root directory_(where your projects src directory lies)_ in vs code. create the file and paste the given map.

version: 0.1

phases:
  pre_build:
    commands:
      - yarn install

  build:
    commands:
      - yarn run build

  post_build:
    commands:
      - aws s3 cp --recursive public-read ./build s3://<bucketname>/
      - aws s3 cp public-read --cache-control="max-age=0, no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate" ./build/index.html s3://<bucketname>/
      - aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id <your-cloudfront-id> --paths /index.html

artifacts:
  files:
    - 'build/*'
    - 'build/**/*'

The YAML file described above is quite simple.

  • In the pre_build stage, the script installs all necessary NPM package dependencies.

  • During the build stage, the script runs the yarn build command to compile and bundle the web app into the build directory.

  • In the post_build phase, the script carries out three operations. Firstly, it copies all contents of the build directory to the designated **S3 **bucket and makes them publicly accessible. Next, it copies the index.html file once more and sets particular caching HTTP headers to prevent the file from being cached by browsers. Finally, it creates an "invalidation" that instructs the **CloudFront **distribution to refresh its content from the S3 source bucket.

Step 6: Push the code to the origin

At this point, we have now finished all the configuration that needs to be done in AWS for CodeBuild to deploy our NextJsapp to S3. So, commit the changes and push the branch to the repository. You will see progress tag when clicking will shows you a deployment progress and after some minutes your code will be deployed to S3. The complete process looks likes this.

Hope you enjoyed the article and learned a new skills today. If you want more from me then don't forget to follow. Also, If you have any queries or feedback then please comment. Thank you and happy deployment!

Items.Title: 将 tRPC 与 REST 结合使用 | Using tRPC with REST
Items.Description:
博士； 我想尝试使用 NextJS 的 tRPC 来对抗 REST，它成功了。 如果您只想要代码，可以在此处找到存储库。

不久前我很好奇并尝试了T3 堆栈，它非常棒。 体验非常完美。 在浏览完文档后，我对它能为我做些什么有了很多想法。

但大多数情况下，我无法控制整个堆栈，只能控制它的前端。 所以使用 T3 堆栈似乎是错误的。 我可以只使用 NextJS。 老实说，我有点沮丧。 我喜欢 tRPC。 我如何在我的 UI 组件中使用这些过程。

我搜索并在 tRPC 网站上为我的用例搜索了某种文档。 但没有运气。 所以我放弃了。

直到今天。 我的女儿正在午睡，tRPC 的想法再次出现在我的脑海中。 好吧，我至少可以尝试模拟一些东西，看看它是如何工作的。 所以我在我的 PowerShell 中输入了命令，然后就开始了。

npm create t3-app@latest

除了 Prisma，我选择了所有的东西。 最重要的是，我迅速添加了一些糖的 Mantine。

这是我构建的应用程序。 一个简单的“电子商务”。 好吧，至少它展示了一些产品。 功能和视觉效果不是这里的重点。

我在网上找到了一个适用于此 POC 的测试 API： Fake store API

我的服务层

我从不在我的组件中获取东西。 我通常为端点访问创建一些服务。 在这个应用程序中，它只是一些用于获取产品的功能。

 export interface Product { id: number; title: string; price: number; category: string; description: string; image: string; rating: Rating; } export interface Rating { rate: number; count: number; } export async function getAllProducts(params: { limit?: string; sort?: "asc" | "desc"; }): Promise<Product[]> { const urlParams = new URLSearchParams({ limit: params.limit ? params.limit : "", sort: params.sort ? params.sort : "asc", }).toString(); const response = await fetch( `https://fakestoreapi.com/products?${urlParams}` ); const data = (await response.json()) as Product[]; return data; }

首先，一些接口来描述数据（来自 fakestoreapi）。 然后是获取数据的函数。

在 repo 中，我有 3 个路由器：Cart、Product 和 User

 export const appRouter = createTRPCRouter({ cart: cartRouter, product: productRouter, user: usersRouter, });

让我们看看产品路由器：

 import { getAllProducts } from "@/services/products.service"; import { createTRPCRouter, publicProcedure } from "../trpc"; import { z } from "zod"; export const productRouter = createTRPCRouter({ getAllProducts: publicProcedure .input( z.object({ limit: z.string().optional(), sort: z.enum(["asc", "desc"]).optional(), }) ) .query(async ({ input }) => { const products = await getAllProducts({ limit: input.limit, sort: input.sort, }); return products; }), });

在这个路由器中，我（为了演示）创建了一个名为 getAllProducts 的过程。 它需要一些可选的输入参数并从服务中获取所有产品。 此 POC 不涉及身份验证，因此它只是一个公共程序。 在现实世界中，可能应该有一个受保护的过程。

在互联网上的所有 tRPC 文档和教程中，查询/变更是您应该进行数据库操作的地方 (Prisma)。 我真的不知道像这样使用它是否是好的做法。 我真的希望这没问题，因为有太多代码我不需要因此而编写。

通常我创建自己的自定义 React-query 挂钩，从我的服务返回数据。 我不必使用 tRPC 执行此操作。 我喜欢它。

界面组件

这就是 tRPC 真正为我做的地方。 看看这段代码：

 const products = api.product.getAllProducts.useQuery({ limit: "8", sort: "asc", });

它很漂亮。 不仅是类型安全，而且我不必为使用该查询编写自己的自定义挂钩。

反腐败层

如果这一切都在服务器上完成，我还可以将该过程用作反腐败层。 我们返回的数据应该是 UI 想要的，而不是端点返回的。 如果后端突然对 API 部署了重大更改并破坏了 UI，会发生什么情况？

在查询中，我们可以（此演示中不存在）将响应映射到具有回退功能的 ui 模型。

结论

我喜欢这种方法。 但我不知道是否有任何回退。 这是你可以使用 tRPC 的方式吗？ 是不是少了什么？ 它会在大型应用程序中很好地扩展吗？ 我想这就像其他一切一样，我将不得不尝试它，评估它并重构如果是这样。

如果可能有另一种方法，或者是否有另一个伟大的、受人尊敬的库而不是 tRPC，请发表评论。



原文:

Tl; Dr; I wanted to try tRPC with NextJS against REST, and it worked. If you just want the code, you can find the repo here.

A while ago I got curious and tried the T3 stack and it was amazing. The experience was just flawless. Prisma, NextJS, NextAuth, and Tailwind together, configured for me, just to start coding. After running through the documentation I had so many ideas as to what it could do for me.

But mostly I don't get to have control of the whole stack, just the front end of it. 99% of the time I get some swagger documentation of all the endpoints available for me.

So using the T3 stack seemed wrong. I could just use NextJS. To be honest, I was kind of bummed. I loved tRPC. How I could use the procedures in my UI components.

I searched and I searched for some kind of documentation on the tRPC website for my use case. But no luck. So I gave it up.

Until today. My daughter was napping and once again the idea of tRPC popped up in my head. Well, I could at least try to mock something up and see how it could work. So I typed in the command in my PowerShell and was on my way.

npm create t3-app@latest

I picked everything except Prisma. On top of that, I quickly added Mantine for some sugar.

This is the application I built. A simple "e-commerce". Well, at least it shows some products. The features and visual is not the point here.

I found a test API online that would work for this POC: Fake store API

My service layer

I never fetch stuff in my components. I typically create some services for endpoint access. In this application, it's just some functions for fetching products.

export interface Product { id: number; title: string; price: number; category: string; description: string; image: string; rating: Rating;
} export interface Rating { rate: number; count: number;
} export async function getAllProducts(params: { limit?: string; sort?: "asc" | "desc";
}): Promise<Product[]> { const urlParams = new URLSearchParams({ limit: params.limit ? params.limit : "", sort: params.sort ? params.sort : "asc", }).toString(); const response = await fetch( `https://fakestoreapi.com/products?${urlParams}` ); const data = (await response.json()) as Product[]; return data;
}

First, some interfaces to describe the data (from the fakestoreapi). Then a function for getting the data.

tRPC Routers

In the repo, I have 3 routers: Cart, Product, and User

export const appRouter = createTRPCRouter({ cart: cartRouter, product: productRouter, user: usersRouter,
});

Let us look at the product router:

import { getAllProducts } from "@/services/products.service";
import { createTRPCRouter, publicProcedure } from "../trpc";
import { z } from "zod"; export const productRouter = createTRPCRouter({ getAllProducts: publicProcedure .input( z.object({ limit: z.string().optional(), sort: z.enum(["asc", "desc"]).optional(), }) ) .query(async ({ input }) => { const products = await getAllProducts({ limit: input.limit, sort: input.sort, }); return products; }),
});

In this router, I (for the demo) create a procedure called getAllProducts. It takes some optional input params and gets all the products from the service. This POC has no authentication involved so it's just a public procedure. In the real world, there should probably be a protected procedure.

In all of the tRPC documentation and tutorials around the internet, the query/mutation is where you are supposed to do database operations (Prisma). I don't really know if it is good practice to use it like this. I really wish this is ok because there is so much code that I don't need to write because of this.

Normally I create my own custom React-query hooks that returns data from my services. I don't have to do that with tRPC. I love it.

UI Component

This is where tRPC really does it for me. Look at this code:

const products = api.product.getAllProducts.useQuery({ limit: "8", sort: "asc",
});

It is just beautiful. Not only the type safety but I didn't have to write my own custom hook for using that query.

Anti corruption layer

If this is all done on the server I can also use the procedure as an anti corruption layer. The data we return should be what the UI want, not what the endpoint returns. What happens if backend suddenly deploys a breaking change to the API and it breaks the UI?

In the query we can (Not present in this demo) map the response to a ui model with fallback.

Conclussion

I like this approach. But I dont know if there are any fallbacks. Is this the way you can use tRPC? Is it something missing? Will it scale well in a large application? I guess it's like everything else, I'll have to try it, evaulate it and refactor if so.

Please leave a comment if there is maybe another approach for this or if there is another great, well respected library instead of tRPC for this cause.
