Items.Description:
穆罕默德·巴尔法奇

发表于 3 月 20 日

  • 什么是面向对象编程 (OOP)？
  • 红宝石和面向对象。
  • 面向对象原则。
    • 抽象。
    • 遗产。
    • 多态性。
    • 封装。

  • 基于“对象”的概念，它可以包含：

    • 数据：它称为字段、属性或属性。
    • 代码：方法也叫程序。

  • 对象的一个​​共同特征是方法附加到它们并且可以访问和修改对象的数据字段。

  • 真实世界的例子：

    • 例子：

     12345.class # => Integer 12345.object_id # => 2469 'abcd'.class # => String 'abcd'.object_id # => 75260 nil.class # => NilClass nil.object_id # => 8 employee = Employee.new # <Employee:0x00000001233c5428 employee.class => # => Employee employee.object_id # => 75240 Employee.object_id # => 72220 Employee.class # => Class
    • 抽象。
    • 遗产。
    • 多态性。
    • 封装。

    • 仅显示类的主要功能并隐藏非必要的实现。

    • 示例：（电视功能）你打开它，然后你会在屏幕上看到输出，无论幕后发生什么（电子电路......等）

    • 在软件开发中：发送电子邮件类（只考虑主要功能，不管其余代码）：

    • 尽可能降低复杂性。

     WeeklyEmail.new(employee).send_mail
     class WeeklyEmail def initialize(employee) self.employee = employee end def send_mail return if employee.email.blank? process_employee_weekly_email end private attr_accessor :employee ... end
    • 另一种抽象形式：
      • 抽象类和方法：
     class ExportBase ... def process_file # Exporting logic that calls exporter_tool method end private def exporter_tool raise NotImplementedError end end class XlsExport < ExportBase def exporter_tool WriteXLSX end ... end class SpreadsheetExport < ExportBase end SpreadsheetExport.new.process_file(file) # will raise the exception
    • 将共享属性和方法放在基类中。
    • 避免重新发明轮子。
    • 例子：
     class Employee ... def delete validate delete_requests ... ... ... remove_from_database end ... end
    • 案例 1：继承实现：
     class Manager < Employee ... end
    • 案例 2：重写实现：（改变行为）
     class Manager < Employee ... def delete validate delete_requests ... ... remove_from_database delete_his_subordinates end ... end
     class Manager < Employee ... def delete super delete_his_subordinates end ... end
    • 具有不同实现的相同方法。
    • 示例：之前的Employee和Manager类，都在使用不同的实现来实现delete方法。
    • 多态性有两种形式：
      • 压倒一切。 例如： Manager类中的先前delete方法。
      • 超载。 您的数据从外部看起来如何。
      • 防止意外访问和修改我们的数据。
      • 在 ruby​​ 中，每个数据（instance_vriable）都是私有的，每个方法都是公共的。
      • 例子：
       class Employee attr_reader :first_name, :last_name def initialize(first_name, last_name, email) @first_name = first_name @last_name = last_name @email = email end def full_name first_name + " " + last_name end end
       employee = Employee.new('Mohammed', 'Balfaqih', 'm.balfaqih@jisr.net') employee.first_name # => "Mohammed" employee.full_name # => "Mohammed Balfaqih" employee.email # NoMethodError (undefined method `email' for #<Employee:0x000000012f9bb9c0>) employee.email = 'hacked-email@gmail.com' # NoMethodError (undefined method `email=' for #<Employee:0x000000012f46d090>)
      • 这里的解决方案是attr_reader & attr_writer (getter & setter)：
       class Employee ... attr_reader :first_name, :last_name attr_writer :email ... end employee.email = 'hacked-email@gmail.com' # => "hacked-email@gmail.com"

      下一个：

      • 超级大战极好的（）
      • 多重继承。
      • 鸭子打字。
      • 模块。

原文:

Mohammed Balfaqih

Posted on Mar 20

  • What is Object Oriented Programming (OOP)?
  • Ruby & OOP.
  • OOP principles.
    • Abstraction.
    • Inheritance.
    • Polymorphism.
    • Encapsulation.

  • OOP is a type of computer programming model that came as a solution for big and more complex code(complexity and size --> they become very difficult to maintain).

  • based on the concept of "objects", which can contains:

    • Data: it called fields, attributes or properties.
    • Code: methods also called procedures.

  • A common feature of objects is that methods are attached to them and can access and modify the object's data fields.

  • Real-world Example:

    • Employee class, it has data (name, email, password, ...) and methods like (view_profile, forgot_password, change_email, ...)

  • Ruby is a pure object-oriented language and everything appears to Ruby as an object.

  • Examples:

12345.class # => Integer
12345.object_id # => 2469 'abcd'.class # => String
'abcd'.object_id # => 75260 nil.class # => NilClass
nil.object_id # => 8 employee = Employee.new # <Employee:0x00000001233c5428
employee.class => # => Employee
employee.object_id # => 75240 Employee.object_id # => 72220
Employee.class # => Class
  • Abstraction.
  • Inheritance.
  • Polymorphism.
  • Encapsulation.

  • Showing only the main functionalities of a class and hiding non-essential implementation.

  • Example: (TV functionality) you switch it On, then you see the output on the screen, No matter what happens behind the scenes (electronic circuits... etc)

  • In software development: sending email class (think only on the main functionality, regardless of the rest of the code):

  • Reduce the complexity as much as we can.

 WeeklyEmail.new(employee).send_mail

 class WeeklyEmail def initialize(employee) self.employee = employee end def send_mail return if employee.email.blank? process_employee_weekly_email end private attr_accessor :employee ... end
  • Another form of Abstraction:
    • Abstract Classes & methods: 
 class ExportBase ... def process_file # Exporting logic that calls exporter_tool method end private def exporter_tool raise NotImplementedError end end class XlsExport < ExportBase def exporter_tool WriteXLSX end ... end class SpreadsheetExport < ExportBase end SpreadsheetExport.new.process_file(file) # will raise the exception
  • Put shared attributes & methods in base class.
  • avoid reinventing the wheel.
  • Example: 
class Employee ... def delete validate delete_requests ... ... ... remove_from_database end ... end
  • case 1: Inherting the implementation: 
class Manager < Employee ... end
  • case 2: Override the implementation: (To change the behavior) 
class Manager < Employee ... def delete validate delete_requests ... ... remove_from_database delete_his_subordinates end ... end

class Manager < Employee ... def delete super delete_his_subordinates end ... end
  • having the same method with different implementations.
  • Example: The previous Employee and Manager classes, both are implementing the delete method with different implementations.
  • There are two forms of polymorphism:
    • overriding. Ex: The previous delete method in Manager class.
    • overloading. Ruby does NOT support overloading.
    • overloading example in java: 
int myMethod(int x)
float myMethod(float x, float y)

overloading example in Ruby: (What will happen??)

class Printer def show(param) puts param end def show(param1, param2) puts param1 puts param2 end end Printer.new.show 'Mohammed'
# => ArgumentError: wrong number of arguments (given 1, expected 2) Printer.new.show 'Mohammed', 'Balfaqih'
# Mohammed
# Balfaqih
# => nil

4- Encapsulation:

  • How your data looks from the outside.
  • Prevents accidentally accessing and modifying our data.
  • In ruby Every data(instance_vriable) is private, and every method is public.
  • Example: 
class Employee attr_reader :first_name, :last_name def initialize(first_name, last_name, email) @first_name = first_name @last_name = last_name @email = email end def full_name first_name + " " + last_name end
end

employee = Employee.new('Mohammed', 'Balfaqih', 'm.balfaqih@jisr.net') employee.first_name
# => "Mohammed" employee.full_name
# => "Mohammed Balfaqih" employee.email
# NoMethodError (undefined method `email' for #<Employee:0x000000012f9bb9c0>) employee.email = 'hacked-email@gmail.com'
# NoMethodError (undefined method `email=' for #<Employee:0x000000012f46d090>)
  • The solution here is attr_reader & attr_writer (getter & setter): 
class Employee
... attr_reader :first_name, :last_name attr_writer :email
...
end employee.email = 'hacked-email@gmail.com'
# => "hacked-email@gmail.com"

Next:

  • super Vs. super()
  • Multiple inheritance.
  • Duck typing.
  • Modules.
